name: Taiga Agile Project Management API
what_it_does: Open-source agile project management platform with REST API for managing Scrum and Kanban projects, user stories, sprints, and team workflows — offering self-hosted and cloud options with full programmatic access.
use_cases: Creating user stories and tasks in Scrum sprints via API from external systems
Querying sprint metrics and burndown data for project reporting agents
Managing Kanban board workflows and card movements programmatically
Integrating Taiga project data with CI/CD pipelines and deployment tracking
Automating backlog grooming by creating and prioritizing user stories via API
not_for: Enterprise project management with advanced reporting (use Jira or Wrike)
Non-agile project management with Gantt/waterfall focus (use OpenProject)
Teams needing strong tool integrations ecosystem (Jira has more integrations)
best_when: A development team needs open-source Scrum/Kanban management with full API access, self-hosting capability, and no per-seat pricing.
avoid_when: You need enterprise project management features, extensive integrations, or dedicated support without self-hosting.

auth.notes: JWT bearer token from login endpoint. Token expires after 24 hours — refresh required. Application tokens for automation (don't expire). Self-hosted instances use same auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: completely free; Cloud: 1 project free
pricing.notes: Mozilla Public License 2.0 open source — fully free to self-host. Cloud adds managed hosting. Very cost-effective alternative to Jira for agile teams. Backed by Kaleidos.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no limits; Cloud: standard rate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documented but older-style documentation. JWT token expiry requires agent refresh logic. Error responses include codes and messages. Webhook delivery for project events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are idempotent. User story creation creates new items — no built-in deduplication. Sprint moves are idempotent for same target sprint.
known_agent_gotchas: JWT token expires after 24 hours — agents must implement refresh logic
Self-hosted documentation may differ from cloud API — verify against your version
Project types (Scrum vs Kanban) have different data models — check project type first
User story points and sprint data models differ from Jira — plan migration carefully

security_score_components.security_notes: Mozilla Public License 2.0 open source — full audit possible. JWT bearer token. TLS enforced on cloud. Self-hosted allows full data control. GDPR compliant. EU-based company.
